title: NOC 40041 – Fire Chiefs and Senior Firefighting Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Fire Chiefs and Senior Firefighting Officers under NOC 40041 are senior leaders responsible for directing firefighting operations and emergency response services across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents top-level command positions within municipal, regional, and industrial fire services. With continued urban development, industrial expansion, and public safety modernization, experienced senior firefighting professionals remain essential to Canada’s emergency management system.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration and public protection servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and protection servicesMinor Group4004 – Managers in public protection servicesNOC Code40041

duties: Fire Chiefs and Senior Firefighting Officers perform some or all of the following:
Develop, implement, and evaluate fire department policies and operational procedures
Plan, direct, and coordinate firefighting strategies
Assess fire incidents, damage levels, and public risk
Direct emergency operations at fire scenes
Investigate fire causes and determine damage estimates
Oversee fire prevention and community safety campaigns
Represent the department in communications with government, media, and the public
Coordinate and monitor departmental budgets and resources
employment_requirements: College diploma in fire protection technology, fire science, or related field (usually required)
Completion of provincial or municipal firefighter training programs
Minimum of 10 years of experience as a firefighter
Fire service certification and promotion requirements vary by province within Canada and are governed by municipal and provincial authorities.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $57,000 to $159,900, depending on rank, jurisdiction, and years of experience.
job_outlook: Advancement to Fire Chief positions typically requires long-term service and progressive leadership within Canadian fire departments.
labour_market_demand: Fire Chiefs and Senior Firefighting Officers are employed in:
Industrial establishments with in-house fire services
Opportunities are particularly available in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
Leadership roles within Canada’s emergency response system
However, most senior firefighting leadership roles require prior Canadian firefighting certification and extensive in-country experience.
pathways: Professionals under NOC 40041 may explore immigration pathways to Canada through:
Because this is a senior public protection role, Canadian certification and extensive local experience are typically required.
